h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep trying to make that 4 count beats too
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to confuse the bottom number of the time signature with a count of beats or a speed marker.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets three tiles in the next bar, then swaps in the eighth-note card over the lower box] Three tiles to this bar line, so the top label stays 3. With this card in the lower box, the lower label changes to 8. No fourth tile gets added.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully manipulates the new beat-unit card and correctly identifies the changing lower number without adding a fourth tile, demonstrating productive action with the device.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical constraint and a concrete failure condition to break the learner's confusion and force a separation of the two numbers.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical covering of the box is a precise, usable device that directly addresses the learner's cognitive overload by isolating the variables.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The worksheet now shows the separation: top number, one bar; bottom number, one beat unit. Mark the next bar with tiles first. Then change only the beat-unit card and say which number can change.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's successful separation and immediately advances the task to changing the beat-unit card, adapting to the learner's new capability.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to identify the other object and place the corresponding card for each arrow in sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
The up arrow is track on cart.
Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the object-first criterion to the final arrow, correctly identifying the track and its upward push without needing step-by-step prompting.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present as there was no need for a strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence:

Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently applies the object-first criterion to the final arrow, correctly identifying the track and its upward push without needing step-by-step prompting.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present as there was no need for a strategy reversal.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Maybe the note should be `rainfall_mm`: recorded rainfall amount in millimeters for that city-month row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will smoothly apply the established row-limit phrasing to the month column and confirm mastery of the pattern.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Testing `April`, it should be one row's month, not a statement about the whole `month` column, but is `city-month row` the right limiter here or is that phrase doing too much?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively stress-tests the newly acquired criterion against a new variable and evaluates the boundaries of the linguistic device itself.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The transcript ends immediately after the learner's pivot turn, leaving no subsequent tutor turn to enact a strategy reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present because the dialogue concludes before the tutor can respond to the learner's pressure.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn to adapt to the learner's revised framing.
